title: New details emerge as Mass. McDonald’s employee accused of giving her ex-girlfriend contaminated fries
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Kaylie Santos, a former McDonald's manager in Southbridge, charged with contaminating fries served to her ex-girlfriend.
Surveillance video reveals Santos allegedly spit into fries before giving them to her ex at the drive-thru.
Santos faces arraignment June 5 and is subject to a no-trespass order from the restaurant's franchise owners.

title: Odds are increasing for a ‘super El Niño’ this summer. Here’s what that means for Mass.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A possible 'super' El Niño could develop in the Pacific, raising Massachusetts temperatures and humidity this summer.
Forecasters predict a reduced risk of Atlantic hurricanes near Massachusetts during a 'super' El Niño event.
Massachusetts may experience warmer winters and more humid summers, though summer effects are generally muted.

title: Billy Eichner talks Conan, Amy Poehler, Madonna – and why he is the way he is
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Billy Eichner's audiobook memoir 'Billy on Billy' releases May 19, sharing stories of his upbringing and career.
The memoir highlights Eichner's supportive relationship with his parents and his love of pop culture.
Eichner will promote the memoir at Boston's Wilbur Theatre and Provincetown, offering a free audiobook with tickets.

title: Michette named among the best French bakeries in the U.S., according to Tasting Table
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Somerville's Michette named one of the 14 best U.S. French bakeries by Tasting Table.
Michette praised for flaky twice-baked croissants and creative cookies by Tasting Table.
Michette joins renowned bakeries from New York, Los Angeles, and New Orleans on Tasting Table's list.

title: Steven Tyler stops for ice cream in Quincy
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Steven Tyler visited Bella's Creamery in Quincy, posing for a photo with a store employee while enjoying ice cream.
Bella's Creamery shared the visit on Facebook, with the post receiving over 3,800 likes by Monday morning.
The Quincy shop where Tyler stopped is located at 652 Washington St., known for their Dave's coffee ice cream.

title: Tom Brady referenced Super Bowl comeback, Bill Belichick jokes in graduation speech
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Tom Brady's graduation speech at Georgetown included stories of his Super Bowl LI comeback win over the Falcons.
Brady used humor about his career, mentioning losses to Eli Manning and jokes about coach Bill Belichick's demanding style.
Brady emphasized perseverance, stating failure is only final when one chooses to quit, urging graduates to embrace challenges.

title: Here’s how much a finance manager makes in Mansfield
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: A 29-year-old finance manager in Mansfield earns $120,000 per year and works hybrid in telecommunications.
His monthly expenses include a $1,100 car payment and a $2,200 mortgage on a home in Cumberland, RI.
He believes Greater Boston needs more housing and improved transportation for the younger workforce.

title: Livestream: Opening statements in rape trial of Alvin Campbell Jr., AG’s brother
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Alvin Campbell Jr., accused of sexually assaulting nine women, faces trial with opening statements underway in Boston.
Campbell, brother of Massachusetts AG Andrea Campbell, allegedly posed as a rideshare driver to assault victims.
Campbell has pleaded not guilty to charges including rape and kidnapping, with the trial delayed since his 2020 arrest.

title: Jaylen Brown curses out Stephen A. Smith, calls on ESPN pundit to retire once again
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Celtics' Jaylen Brown criticized Stephen A. Smith on Twitch, urging the ESPN pundit to retire for using 'clickbait' journalism.
Brown played clips of Smith's criticism from 'First Take', objecting to comments on the 2025-26 NBA season and Jayson Tatum.
Brown refuted Smith's claims, defending his remarks on the Celtics' season and emphasizing team growth despite playoff exit.

title: Condo in CT’s once-controversial ‘spaceship’ building lists for 779k
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: The one-bedroom, two-bathroom condo known as 'The Spaceship' in Guilford, CT, is listed for $779,000.
Designed by Wilfred Armster in the 1980s, the building received criticism for its non-traditional style.
The condo features views of Long Island Sound, hardwood floors, and modern amenities, including a two-car garage.
